old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-u7d","title":"Implement Google OAuth 2.0 backend","description":"Implement direct Google OAuth 2.0 / OIDC flow in Go using golang.org/x/oauth2. Endpoints: GET /api/auth/login (redirect to Google), GET /api/auth/callback (exchange code, upsert user record, set signed HTTP-only session cookie), POST /api/auth/logout (clear cookie). Any Google account can sign up (open). Store user in SQLite users table on first login.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:05Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:05Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-tam","title":"Investigate and define testing strategy for the project","description":"The initial unit/integration test approach was closed without merging. Need to decide on the right testing pattern before writing any tests.

Consider:
- E2E tests: test the full stack (browser to backend)
- Component testing: test individual frontend components in isolation
- Contract testing: verify API contracts between frontend and backend
- Unit tests: keep only for genuinely complex functions (business logic, parsing)

Deliverables: decision doc with chosen strategy and rationale, example test(s) demonstrating the pattern, and guidelines on when unit tests are appropriate vs the chosen higher-level approach.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-15T19:35:04Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-15T19:35:04Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-clu","title":"Add validate tags and validator calls to UpdateTaskRequest and AddSymbolRequest","description":"M1+M2: Two handler DTOs are missing go-playground/validator struct tags and validator.Struct() calls, inconsistent with CreateTaskRequest which was correctly updated.

1. dto.go UpdateTaskRequest: add validate tags:
   Text *string validate:"omitempty,min=1,max=1000"
   Priority *string validate:"omitempty,oneof=high medium low"
   Then call taskValidator.Struct(&req) in the Update handler after decode, return 400 on failure.

2. stocks_dto.go AddSymbolRequest: add validate tags:
   Symbol string validate:"required,min=1,max=20"
   Then call validator.Struct(&body) in AddSymbol handler after decode (reuse the existing taskValidator or create a shared one), return 400 on failure.

Both fixes are in the same handler package and can ship in a single PR.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:23Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:23Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-9ya","title":"Service layer hardening: url.QueryEscape, io.LimitReader, errors.Is","description":"M3+M4+M5: Three small hardening fixes across the service and repository layers, all shippable in one PR.

M3 (service/stocks.go:193): Symbol not URL-encoded in Finnhub quote URL. Change:
  fmt.Sprintf("https://finnhub.io/api/v1/quote?symbol=%s", symbol)
to:
  fmt.Sprintf("https://finnhub.io/api/v1/quote?symbol=%s", url.QueryEscape(symbol))
SearchSymbols already does this correctly — apply the same pattern to fetchFinnhub.

M4 (service/news.go:92): io.ReadAll on upstream response body with no size limit. Change to:
  io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 1<<20)) — consistent with stocks service.

M5 (repository/sqlite_task_repository.go:95): sql.ErrNoRows compared with == instead of errors.Is. Change to errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) for future-proofing against wrapped errors.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:28Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:28Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-9cq","title":"Infrastructure and config hardening","description":"LOW/infrastructure items that can ship together:

1. CORS origin hardcoded to localhost:5173 (middleware/cors.go:10). Make configurable via CORS_ORIGIN env var, defaulting to http://localhost:5173. Update config.go to read it and wire into cors middleware. Update .env.example and README env vars table.

2. Add govulncheck to CI/CD pipeline (if one exists) or document as a manual step in CLAUDE.md/README. Run: go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest && govulncheck ./...

3. Dashboard goroutine concurrent struct writes (dashboard.go:57-127): Add a comment above var resp documenting the safety invariant — each goroutine writes to a distinct field, g.Wait() provides the happens-before guarantee for the final read. Low-risk but documents the assumption for future contributors.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:41Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-16T01:14:41Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-frq","title":"Harden Go backend HTTP layer","description":"Create shared internal packages to eliminate boilerplate and inconsistency across handlers and services:
1. internal/response/ package — WriteJSON(w, status, v) and WriteError(w, status, msg) helpers to replace repeated json.NewEncoder patterns and silent encode-error discards across all handlers
2. internal/httpclient/ or service-level helper — shared pattern for external API calls (set headers, io.LimitReader body, unmarshal JSON) currently duplicated across weather, news, stocks, commute, sunrise, quotes services
3. Centralize sentinel errors — ErrNotFound, ErrInvalidInput etc. in a shared package so handlers can use errors.Is() consistently
4. Shared validator instance — single go-playground/validator/v10 instance in internal/validate/ or similar, reused across all handlers
Goal: reduce per-handler boilerplate, ensure consistent HTTP status codes and response format across all endpoints.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-16T01:00:33Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-16T01:00:33Z"}

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-lmc","title":"Fix label picker: clicks not registering on dropdown items","description":"Label picker dropdown opens correctly but clicking label items to assign them does nothing. Root causes: (1) mousedown outside-click handler may close picker during concurrent re-renders since pickerRef.current.contains() could fail; (2) no optimistic update on assignLabel means zero immediate visual feedback. Fix: add onMouseDown stopPropagation on dropdown div, add zIndex to open task row, add optimistic update to assignLabel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:58:00Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-20T03:58:00Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-0gm","title":"Fix AssignLabel SQL: ON CONFLICT clause incompatible with partial unique index","description":"AssignLabel repository method uses 'ON CONFLICT(task_id, label_id) DO UPDATE' but the unique constraint on task_label_assignments is a partial unique index (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL) created separately, not an inline UNIQUE constraint in CREATE TABLE. SQLite's ON CONFLICT (columns) clause only recognizes PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE constraints defined in the table definition, not standalone CREATE UNIQUE INDEX. Result: every POST /api/tasks/{id}/labels returns 500. Fix: replace ON CONFLICT upsert with plain INSERT — service layer already guards against duplicates.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T04:06:36Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-20T04:06:36Z"}

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-75c","title":"Implement EncryptionService for calendar_ics_url in user_settings","description":"HIGH security finding from PR #29 review. The calendar_ics_url column stores ICS feed URLs which frequently contain long-lived auth tokens (Google Calendar private feed URLs, Outlook tokens). These are effectively bearer credentials and must NOT be stored in plaintext. Implement an EncryptionService in backend/internal/service/encryption.go with: Encrypt(plaintext string) (ciphertext string, error) and Decrypt(ciphertext string) (plaintext string, error) using AES-GCM with a key derived from a new ENCRYPTION_KEY env var. Inject into the user settings service. Encrypt on write, decrypt on read. Update .env.example and README with the new env var.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:37:09Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:37:09Z"}

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-gsd","title":"Add SSRF scheme validation for calendar_ics_url on backend","description":"The backend fetches calendar_ics_url without validating the scheme. A user (or attacker with access) could supply file://, ftp://, or internal network URLs (e.g. http://169.254.169.254/). {"id":"argus-3wd","title":"Notification Center: DB migrations (Phase 1)","description":"Create 3 isolated DB migrations for the notification center feature. Must ship in its own PR per migration isolation convention.

Files to create:
- backend/migrations/010_create_user_integrations.sql
- backend/migrations/011_create_user_watched_repos.sql
- backend/migrations/012_create_notifications.sql

Schema details in plan: .omc/plans/notification-center.md (Phase 1 section)

Key points:
- user_integrations: id, user_id, provider, access_token (encrypted), refresh_token, provider_user_id, provider_username, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at. UNIQUE(user_id, provider).
- user_watched_repos: id, user_id, integration_id, owner, repo, webhook_id, webhook_secret (encrypted), created_at, deleted_at. INDEX on (owner, repo).
- notifications: id, user_id, provider, event_type, title, body, url, read_at, dismissed_at, github_delivery_id (UNIQUE, prevents webhook retry duplicates), created_at. Partial indexes on (user_id, created_at DESC).

Acceptance criteria:
- All 3 migrations have +goose Up and +goose Down
- PKs are TEXT PRIMARY KEY (UUID v7 generated in app)
- go run ./cmd/server starts and runs migrations cleanly
- goose down cleanly drops all 3 tables

{"id":"argus-mmz","title":"Notification Center: Add provider_types and notification_event_types lookup tables (schema fix)","description":"Migrations 010-012 used CHECK constraints for 'provider' and 'event_type' columns. Project convention requires lookup tables (like bill_category_types, task_priorities). Fix with 3 new migrations:

- 013_create_provider_types.sql — lookup table with seed row: github
- 014_create_notification_event_types.sql — lookup table with 5 seed rows: pr_opened, pr_merged, pr_closed, pr_comment, pr_review_comment

Must be isolated PR per migration isolation convention.

{"id":"argus-ta1","title":"Notification Center: Repository layer (Phase 2b)","description":"Second sub-task of the backend phase (argus-5a6). Pure SQL CRUD — no business logic. Depends on Phase 2a (models and errors must exist).

Files to create:
- backend/internal/repository/sqlite_integration_repository.go — implements IntegrationStore interface (defined in service/github_integration.go in Phase 2e). Methods: Create, GetByUserAndProvider, GetByID, SoftDelete
- backend/internal/repository/sqlite_watched_repo_repository.go — implements WatchedRepoStore interface. Methods: Create, ListByIntegration, GetByOwnerRepo, SoftDelete
- backend/internal/repository/sqlite_notification_repository.go — implements NotificationStore interface. Methods: Create, List (paginated, filterable by state), GetByID, MarkRead, MarkDismissed, MarkAllRead, CountUnread

Key rules:
- No SELECT * — explicit columns only
- All queries scoped to user_id
- Repository Delete/SoftDelete returns (int64, error); never returns ErrNotFound
- Service layer (Phase 2c/2e) checks rowsAffected==0 and maps to domain error
- Use sqlx.DB, follow pattern from existing repositories (e.g. internal/repository/sqlite_task_repository.go)

Plan reference: .omc/plans/notification-center.md (Phase 2B section)
Parent epic: argus-5a6 (backend sub-epic), argus-2es (main epic)

Acceptance criteria:
- go build ./... passes
- golangci-lint run ./... passes
- All queries use explicit column lists
- Each repository file has matching store interface defined (or placeholder interface ready for service layer)

{"id":"argus-iy3","title":"Notification Center: Notification service + handler (Phase 2c)","description":"Third sub-task of the backend phase (argus-5a6). Implements the notification CRUD service and REST handler. Depends on Phase 2b (repositories must exist).

Files to create:
- backend/internal/service/notification.go — NotificationStore interface + NotificationService. Methods: List (paginated, state filter: unread/read/dismissed/all), GetByID, MarkRead, MarkDismissed, MarkAllRead, CountUnread
- backend/internal/handler/notifications.go — AddRoutes registers: GET /api/notifications, PATCH /api/notifications/:id, POST /api/notifications/mark-all-read. All routes in requireAuth group.
- backend/internal/handler/notifications_dto.go — ListNotificationsRequest (limit, offset, state query params), NotificationResponse, PatchNotificationRequest (action: 'read'|'dismissed')

- backend/internal/model/models.go — add UnreadNotifications int to DashboardResponse
- backend/internal/handler/dashboard.go — add CountUnread goroutine to errgroup (fails gracefully, zero value on error)
- backend/internal/server/server.go — wire NotificationRepository → NotificationService → NotificationHandler; call notificationH.AddRoutes(protected)

Key rules:
- Rate limit PATCH and POST endpoints via httprate.LimitByIP in AddRoutes
- All logging via log/slog
- Pagination: default 20, max 100 (use constants from handler/constants.go)
- State filter: 'unread' (default), 'read', 'dismissed', 'all'
- PATCH action 'read' sets read_at; 'dismissed' sets dismissed_at

Plan reference: .omc/plans/notification-center.md (Phase 2C section)

{"id":"argus-miv","title":"Notification Center: Webhook handler (Phase 2d)","description":"Fourth sub-task of the backend phase (argus-5a6). Implements HMAC-validated webhook receiver and GitHub event parser. Security-critical — deserves isolated review. Depends on Phase 2c (notification service must exist to create notifications).

Files to create:
- backend/internal/service/webhook.go — ValidateHMACSignature(secret, payload, sigHeader) bool; ParseGitHubEvent(eventType, payload) (*NotificationCreate, error). Event mapping: pull_request (opened/closed+merged/closed) → pr_opened/pr_merged/pr_closed; issue_comment created on PR → pr_comment; pull_request_review_comment created → pr_review_comment. Returns nil for unhandled events (no notification created).
- backend/internal/handler/webhooks.go — AddRoutes registers: POST /api/webhooks/github on PUBLIC router (not requireAuth). Reads X-Hub-Signature-256, X-GitHub-Event, X-GitHub-Delivery headers. Looks up watched repo by owner+repo from payload. Validates HMAC. Parses event. Creates notification via NotificationService. POST /api/webhooks/github/_simulate — only registered when AppEnv == 'development'; skips HMAC validation.
- backend/internal/handler/webhooks_dto.go — GitHubWebhookPayload (pull_request, issue_comment, pull_request_review_comment shapes); SimulatePayload for dev endpoint

Key rules (SECURITY CRITICAL):
- HMAC validation uses crypto/hmac + crypto/sha256; constant-time comparison via hmac.Equal
- Look up webhook secret from user_watched_repos by (owner, repo); decrypt before HMAC comparison
- Return 200 OK even for unhandled events (GitHub retries on non-2xx)
- Return 400 only for malformed JSON; 401 for invalid signature
- _simulate endpoint MUST check AppEnv == 'development' before registering; never active in production
- Store X-GitHub-Delivery as github_delivery_id; duplicate delivery (UNIQUE constraint violation) → return 200 silently
- All logging via log/slog

Plan reference: .omc/plans/notification-center.md (Phase 2D section)
Parent epic: argus-5a6 (backend sub-epic), argus-2es (main epic)

Acceptance criteria:
- go build ./... passes
- golangci-lint run ./... passes
- Valid HMAC payload creates a notification in DB
- Invalid HMAC returns 401
- Unhandled event type returns 200 with no notification created
- Duplicate X-GitHub-Delivery returns 200 silently (idempotent)
- _simulate endpoint only available when APP_ENV=development

{"id":"argus-8so","title":"Notification Center: GitHub integration service + handler (Phase 2e)","description":"Fifth and final sub-task of the backend phase (argus-5a6). Implements GitHub OAuth link flow, repo listing, and webhook auto-install/remove. Most complex sub-task. Depends on Phase 2d (full service/handler patterns established).

Files to create:
- backend/internal/service/github_integration.go — IntegrationStore + WatchedRepoStore interfaces. GitHubIntegrationService methods: GetAuthURL(state) string, ExchangeCode(ctx, code) (*UserIntegration, error), ListUserRepos(ctx, userID) ([]GitHubRepo, error), UpdateWatchedRepos(ctx, userID, repoFullNames []string) error (diff current vs desired: install webhooks for new, remove for deselected), Disconnect(ctx, userID) error (best-effort: remove all webhooks, soft-delete integration + watched repos)
- backend/internal/handler/github_auth.go — GET /api/auth/github (requireAuth: generate state cookie, redirect to GitHub). GET /api/auth/github/callback (requireAuth: exchange code, link token to session user, redirect to frontend /settings)
- backend/internal/handler/integrations.go — AddRoutes: DELETE /api/integrations/github (requireAuth), GET /api/integrations/github/repos (requireAuth), PUT /api/integrations/github/repos (requireAuth)
- backend/internal/handler/integrations_dto.go — RepoItem{FullName, Watched bool}, UpdateWatchedReposRequest{Repos []string}

Files to modify:
- backend/internal/server/server.go — wire IntegrationRepository + WatchedRepoRepository → GitHubIntegrationService → integrationH + githubAuthH; register routes
- .env.example — add GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET, GITHUB_CALLBACK_URL, APP_ENV
- README.md — document new env vars, GitHub OAuth App setup steps, webhook testing with gh webhook forward and _simulate endpoint

Key rules:
- OAuth is LINK-ONLY: callback is in requireAuth group; links token to existing session user; never creates new user
- OAuth scopes: read:user repo admin:repo_hook
- Webhook events to subscribe: [pull_request, issue_comment, pull_request_review_comment]
- Tokens encrypted via EncryptionService before storage; decrypted on read
- Webhook secret: crypto/rand 32 bytes, hex-encoded, encrypted before storage
- Disconnect is best-effort: log GitHub API errors, always soft-delete local rows
- Rate limit PUT and DELETE via httprate.LimitByIP

Plan reference: .omc/plans/notification-center.md (Phase 2E section)
Parent epic: argus-5a6 (backend sub-epic), argus-2es (main epic)

Acceptance criteria:
- go build ./... passes
- golangci-lint run ./... passes
- GET /api/auth/github redirects to GitHub OAuth (requires session)
- Callback links GitHub token to logged-in user; unauthenticated callback returns 401
- GET /api/integrations/github/repos returns user repo list with watched status
- PUT /api/integrations/github/repos installs/removes webhooks for diff
- DELETE /api/integrations/github removes all webhooks and soft-deletes integration
- .env.example and README.md updated with new vars
